name: Serbian

formality.description: Serbian has a T-V distinction: Ви (Vi, formal/plural) vs ти (ti, informal/singular). Professional software uses Ви-form. Serbian is written in both Cyrillic (official) and Latin scripts — rosetta translates to Latin script and offers a deterministic Cyrillic converter.

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Serbian has grammatical gender. When gender is unknown, masculine is the conventional default.

formal-vi.label: Ви (Formal)
formal-vi.description: Standard for professional software. Output in Latin script, optionally converted to Cyrillic.
formal-vi.prompt: Formal Serbian. Use Ви-form for second-person address. Professional register. Translate in Latin script — Cyrillic conversion is handled automatically by the script converter if enabled.

informal-ti.label: Ти (Informal)
informal-ti.description: For consumer apps and casual contexts.
informal-ti.prompt: Informal Serbian. Use ти-form. Conversational tone. Translate in Latin script.

notes: Serbian uses both Latin and Cyrillic scripts interchangeably. The translation pipeline works in Latin, then the deterministic Latin→Cyrillic converter (scripts.js) can produce the Cyrillic variant.
